scope.summary: Routes a locked setup --write through the running service's reviewed mTLS config API without weakening the service-owner flock.
out_of_scope: Changing the service-owner flock into a per-operation lock.
Provisioning a live control listener, operator certificate, or authorized mTLS subject.
Proving B1a control-listener admission or a live Oracle environment.

entry_points.name: oraclemcp --json setup --write with an owned state store
entry_points.trace: C6 holds the real FileStore service-owner flock, configures an mTLS control listener, and proves the CLI sends the reviewed draft request followed by an apply request carrying the same draft, preview token, digest, and confirmation.

entry_points.name: running service operator config API
entry_points.trace: The fallback accepts only an HTTPS origin and explicit CA/client certificate/key inputs, verifies the configured server identity, rejects a remote target path that differs from the requested config, and invokes /operator/v1/config/draft then /operator/v1/config/apply.

entry_points.name: offline state-mutating CLI collision
entry_points.trace: C6 retains the two existing collision checks: direct setup/client mutations do not report success under the held lock, and a fallback setup failure remains actionable rather than generic.

limitations: Scoped offline verification passed: cargo fmt -p oraclemcp -- --check; cargo test -p oraclemcp --test c6_cli_vs_running_server -- --nocapture (3 passed); cargo test -p oraclemcp --bin oraclemcp -- --nocapture (133 passed).

C6 is an mTLS wire-contract fixture, not a live served-oraclemcp or live Oracle proof; it verifies the lock-preserving client handoff and reviewed request binding, not B1a's real control-listener admission or a live reload result.
cargo clippy -p oraclemcp --bin oraclemcp -- -D warnings remains blocked before this change reaches A2c by crates/oraclemcp-db/src/intelligence.rs:1493, clippy::too_many_arguments on get_source.
